title: French Present Tense Conjugation
description: Regular -er/-ir/-re endings, real -ir families, spelling changes, and high-frequency irregular verbs

crashCourse.title: Present tense conjugation — quick lesson
crashCourse.subtitle: The present tense is not just “regular endings.” Learn the main verb families first, especially the different -ir patterns.

sections.title: Step 1 — Start with the subject
sections.content: French endings change with the subject: je, tu, il/elle/on, nous, vous, ils/elles. The silent endings are the trap: parle, parles, parlent sound similar but are written differently.
sections.tip: Always ask: who is doing the action? Then choose the matching ending.

sections.title: Step 2 — Regular -er verbs
sections.content: Most French verbs are regular -er verbs. Drop -er, then add -e, -es, -e, -ons, -ez, -ent.
sections.tip: parler → je parle, tu parles, il parle, nous parlons, vous parlez, ils parlent.

sections.title: Step 3 — Spelling-change -er verbs
sections.content: These still use regular -er endings, but spelling changes preserve pronunciation.
sections.tip: -ger: nous mangeons. -cer: nous commençons. acheter/préférer: j’achète, je préfère. appeler: j’appelle. -yer: je nettoie, je paie.

sections.title: Step 4 — Regular 2nd-group -ir verbs
sections.content: This is the “finir” family. It is regular, but it does NOT include all -ir verbs. Drop -ir and add -is, -is, -it, -issons, -issez, -issent.
sections.tip: If the plural has -iss- — nous finissons, vous choisissez, ils réussissent — it is this regular 2nd-group pattern.

sections.title: Step 5 — The other -ir verbs: partir / dormir / sortir family
sections.content: Many common -ir verbs are 3rd-group verbs, not regular finir-style verbs. A major family is partir, sortir, dormir, servir, sentir, mentir.
sections.tip: Singular often drops the final stem consonant: je pars, tu dors, il sort. Plural keeps it: nous partons, vous dormez, ils sortent.

sections.title: Step 6 — The -ir verbs that behave like -er: ouvrir / offrir / couvrir
sections.content: Ouvrir, offrir, couvrir, souffrir, découvrir are -ir verbs, but in the present they mostly take -er-style endings.
sections.tip: j’ouvre, tu offres, il couvre, nous ouvrons, vous offrez, ils découvrent — NOT j’ouvris or nous ouvrissons.

sections.title: Step 7 — Venir / tenir family
sections.content: Venir and tenir, plus compounds like devenir, revenir, obtenir, have their own pattern.
sections.tip: je viens, tu viens, il vient, nous venons, vous venez, ils viennent. tenir → je tiens, nous tenons, ils tiennent.

sections.title: Step 8 — Regular -re verbs
sections.content: For regular -re verbs, drop -re and add -s, -s, nothing, -ons, -ez, -ent.
sections.tip: vendre → je vends, tu vends, il vend, nous vendons, vous vendez, ils vendent.

sections.title: Step 9 — High-frequency irregular verbs
sections.content: These appear constantly and need memorization. Focus on the forms that break expectations.
sections.tip: être, avoir, aller, faire, prendre, mettre, dire, lire, écrire, voir, boire, recevoir, devoir, pouvoir, vouloir, savoir.

rows: je suis, tu es, il est, nous sommes, vous êtes, ils sont
j’ai, tu as, il a, nous avons, vous avez, ils ont
je vais, nous allons, ils vont
je fais, vous faites, ils font
je prends, nous prenons, ils prennent
je mets, nous mettons, ils mettent
je dis, vous dites, ils disent
je vois, nous voyons, ils voient
je bois, nous buvons, ils boivent
je reçois, nous recevons, ils reçoivent
je peux, nous pouvons, ils peuvent
je veux, nous voulons, ils veulent

sections.title: Step 10 — How to approach the quiz
sections.content: Do not rely only on the infinitive ending. First identify the family: regular -er, spelling-change -er, regular finir-style -ir, irregular -ir family, regular -re, or memorized irregular.
sections.tip: The biggest trap: “-ir” does not mean one rule. finir, dormir, ouvrir, venir, courir, mourir, and fuir all behave differently.

sections.title: Part 1: Regular and spelling-change patterns
sections.instructions: Choose the correct present-tense form. This section covers regular -er, 2nd-group -ir, regular -re, and spelling-change verbs.

questions.explanation: Regular -er; keep the i in nous/vous: nous étudions.
acheter changes e → è before silent endings.
appeler doubles l before silent endings.
payer commonly changes y → i before silent endings.
-oyer verbs change y → i before silent endings.

sections.title: Part 2: Irregular -ir families

sections.title: Part 4: Spot and fix the present-tense error

questions.hint: partir is not a finir-style verb.
ouvrir conjugates like -er in the present.

questions.sentence: Il mourit à la fin du film.
questions.correct: Il meurt à la fin du film.
